mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6.3% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting central banks to adopt accommodative policies that drive S&P 500 forward returns negatively over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.3%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and GDP signals strong supply-side momentum, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: The joint movement of INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to predict SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.15 over any 3-year rolling window, or individual series show no significant correlation with SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising federal funds rates, increase discount rates for equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25% quarter-over-quarter, or the directional correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 236.25 (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing real interest rates and reducing equity valuations, which drives S&P 500 forward returns negatively over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 236.25, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls and declining unemployment signals a robust labor market, which drives S&P 500 forward returns positively over the next 60 trading days through increased consumer spending.
failure_condition: The joint movement of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.15 over any 3-year rolling window, or individual series show no significant correlation with SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved sectoral performance.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of periods where HOUST rises by > 5% month-over-month, or the correlation between HOUST and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) during late-cycle expansions signal sustained consumer demand, which drives S&P 500 forward returns positively over the next 20 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the correlation between RSAFS and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
